selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 9.3% and 20-day momentum is 6.2%.
Trend quality is supported by 30% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 4.7% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 2.1%, downside volatility near 1.3%, and a recent max drawdown of 6.2%.
Volume confirmation is 29.5% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 11.8% versus SPY over 5 days and 5.8% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.86, confidence 0.82, and 0.8 weighted articles across 3 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.86, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 3 sources, 0.8 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.94, average source quality 0.75, confidence 0.82.
Broad market news leaned bullish for the Healthcare sector with score 0.68 and confidence 0.51.

A rotation into "quality" ETFs channels incremental asset flows into stocks with stable earnings, strong balance sheets, and dividend/quality characteristics. Healthcare stocks often qualify as quality/durable-earnings names, so they tend to receive relative inflows and modest upside to valuations in the short run. The effect is driven by ETF and passive flows and investor preference shifts rather than company-specific news, so impacts are sector-level and modest over the next 1–2 weeks.
Sector overlay was trimmed by 35% because it overlaps with company or macro themes.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.00).
Overall model score is 0.224 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Medtronic plc (MDT) Acquires SPR Therapeutics to Enhance Pain Intervention Prospects
news_evidence.llm_reason: Reports Medtronic's acquisition of SPR Therapeutics — a direct company action affecting product portfolio (pain intervention/neurostimulation). Likely positive for strategic growth and investor sentiment; size/terms unknown so near-term market impact is moderate.
